fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Pennsylvania Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer pharmacist license to pennsylvania, pharmacist compact pennsylvania, pennsylvania pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: Pennsylvania State Board of Pharmacy, P.O. Box 2649, Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Create PALS Account & Initiate Application
steps.description: Register for an account on the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS) at pals.pa.gov. Log in and select 'New Application', then choose 'Pharmacist by Endorsement' to start your online application.

steps.title: Submit NABP e-Profile ID & Verifications
steps.description: Obtain an NABP e-Profile ID and use the NABP's Electronic Licensure Transfer Program (ELTP) to request official verification of all out-of-state pharmacist licenses be sent directly to the PA Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass the Pennsylvania MPJE
steps.description: Register for and pass the Pennsylvania Multistate Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) through NABP, ensuring your scores are officially submitted to the PA Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Checks
steps.description: Register with IdentoGO (PA's authorized vendor) to complete the required state and federal criminal history record checks, ensuring the results are electronically sent to the Board.

steps.title: Provide Supporting Documentation & Photo
steps.description: Upload a current passport-style photo directly within the PALS application. Arrange for official transcripts from your ACPE-accredited pharmacy program to be sent directly to the Board, if not already submitted.

steps.title: Pay Fees & Await Board Review
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee through the PALS portal using a credit/debit card. Monitor your PALS account for application status updates and license issuance once all required documents are received and reviewed by the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Pennsylvania pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Pennsylvania, candidates must submit their application and all supporting documentation through the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS) online portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit pals.pa.gov. Click the 'Register' or 'New User' button to create a new PALS account. Follow the prompts to enter your personal information, set up login credentials, and verify your email address. Once registered, log in to your PALS account and select 'New Application' to begin your pharmacist license application.
